signals.text: Monthly connect call completed Apr 14 with Seth Baron and Liz Snyder from Verb agency - first meeting with new agency contacts went well
Pulkit proactively reached out Apr 6 about TikTok funnel traffic surge, proposing strategy optimization call
Data discrepancy identified: TikTok showed $47K purchase value vs FERMAT showing only $239.96 - attribution model mismatch explained to agency
Contract transitioned from annual to month-to-month at $2K/month via Stripe on Mar 13 due to internal restructuring
High stakeholder turnover: CSM changed from Mia to Kaitlin to Pulkit; agency changed from Chief Detective/SarahJay to Verb/Seth and Liz
Next monthly connect already scheduled for May 14 - regular cadence maintained
FERMAT metrics API authentication unavailable - unable to score platform performance this refresh cycle

recent.title: FlavCity x FERMAT | Monthly Connect
recent.participants: Seth Baron (seth@verb.tech), Elizabeth Snyder (liz@verb.tech), Pulkit Srivastava, Nidhi Singh
recent.summary: First meeting with Verb agency (Seth and Liz) managing FlavCity TikTok and paid media. Key topic: data discrepancy between TikTok ($47K purchase value for butter coffee) and FERMAT ($239.96) due to attribution model differences. Recommended using TikTok/Meta reports as primary data source. Shared upcoming Claude/MCP integration and funnel building features.
action_items: Share help articles on folder organization and MCP setup

recent.title: Flavcity x Fermat
recent.summary: Contract transition call. FlavCity requested month-to-month due to internal changes with contract expiring Apr 15. Agreed to $2K/month via Stripe. Mia announced transition to product team. Will expressed continued interest in AI Search product.
action_items: Reply to Christy's email with month-to-month details

recent.title: FlavCity x Fermat Weekly
recent.participants: Christy (christy@shopflavcity.com), Ryan Longo (ryan@chiefdetective.com), Kaitlin Frye, Mia DyBuncio
recent.summary: CSM transition from Mia to Kaitlin. Christy (new D2C lead) introduced. Strategy shifted from try-it-kits to full-price bags focusing on breakfast replacement and GLP1 themes. Plan to create enhanced PDPs and listicle-style funnels matching ad angles. Ryan to share marketing calendar and winning headlines.
action_items: Kaitlin to schedule platform walkthrough with Christy
Ryan to share marketing calendar and winning ad headlines

calls.analysis: FlavCity has been through significant transition: CSM changed from Mia to Kaitlin to now Pulkit, and the agency shifted from Chief Detective to Verb. The Apr 14 call with Verb was productive but surfaced a data attribution discrepancy that needs clarification. The brand moved to month-to-month billing as of Mar 13, indicating some flexibility risk, though continued engagement via monthly calls is a positive sign.

slack.analysis: No Slack MCP available for this refresh.

messages.subject: FlavCity x FERMAT — Loving the TikTok Traffic, Let's Chat Strategy
messages.snippet: Hey Team, Hope all is well on your end! Wanted to reach out because we noticed something pretty wild on our side. Your TikTok funnels on Fermat have been getting a massive amount of traffic lately. We...

email.analysis: Communication is active with Pulkit proactively reaching out on Apr 6 about TikTok traffic surge. The Verb agency contacts (Seth and Liz) accepted the Apr 14 meeting invitation promptly. Email thread shows good engagement cadence, though most recent substantive email to the brand was Apr 6 (9 days ago).
